Gabriel Núñez-Sánchez

Tax Intern at RSM Puerto Rico  ·  B.D. Finance & Accounting, UPR

Finance and Accounting student with hands-on tax compliance and bookkeeping experience at RSM Puerto Rico, a leading national firm. Proven leader as President of the Financial Management Association (72 members) and Director of the Investment Club. 01

Professional Experience

🏢
Tax Intern
RSM Puerto Rico
June 2025 – Present ▼

RSM is a top-tier national public accounting firm. As a Tax Intern, I work across a broad range of compliance engagements under Puerto Rico tax law.

  • Prepared PR income tax returns for individuals (Form 482), corporations (Form 480.2), trusts (Form 480.8F), and pass-through entities (Form 480.20).
  • Prepared Property Tax Returns (PPT) and filed informative returns (Forms 480.6A, 480.6SP, 480.7E).
  • Managed QuickBooks bookkeeping and bank reconciliations for multiple clients simultaneously.
  • Represented clients before the Puerto Rico Department of Treasury (Hacienda) in compliance matters.
  • Prepared Annual Reports and Exempt Annual Reports for various entities.
📊
Accounting Intern
César Muñiz & Co PSC
Jan 2025 – June 2025 ▼

Contributed to audit and tax compliance workflows at a local CPA firm, working with a diverse client portfolio.

  • Performed bank reconciliations using Sage for 15+ clients, ensuring accuracy of financial records.
  • Assisted in materiality analysis during audit verification processes.
  • Processed informative tax forms (480.6A, 480.6SP, 480.7E) for clients across multiple industries.
  • Prepared documentation for annual reports, supporting timely regulatory filings.
📐
Mathematics Tutor
University of Puerto Rico – Bayamón
Sep 2022 – Dec 2023 ▼

Provided academic support to university students in mathematics, applying analytical communication skills and creating supplementary learning resources.

  • Led two student groups focused on break-even analysis, working with 7–10 students per session.
  • Achieved 70% A/B grade outcomes among tutored students through targeted instruction.
  • Created a YouTube channel with supplementary content to reinforce concepts outside classroom hours.
🍦
Customer Service Representative
Paletados
2022 – 2025 ▼

Paletados is a gourmet ice cream and paleta company known for its presence at high-profile corporate and sporting events in Puerto Rico. As a customer service representative, I consistently went above and beyond, becoming the company's top-reviewed employee.

  • Delivered exceptional customer service at corporate events, sporting events, and large-scale festivals, representing the brand with professionalism.
  • Became the highest-reviewed employee — multiple customers wrote directly to the company on Google to recognize the outstanding service they received.
  • Participated in the Feria Bacardí, one of Puerto Rico's premier events, selling paletas and engaging with hundreds of attendees.
  • Featured in a television commercial for Paletados, showcasing dedication and brand ambassadorship that went beyond the standard role.
  • Demonstrated reliability and initiative, consistently exceeding expectations and contributing to the company's reputation for quality service.
02

Leadership & Campus

🏆
President
Financial Management Association (FMA) – UPR Bayamón
72 Members ▼

As President of the Financial Management Association at UPR Bayamón, I led a student organization of 72 members, transforming it into an active platform for professional development, industry networking, and financial education. My role spanned strategy, event planning, corporate relations, and member development.

  • Directed an organization of 72 active members, setting the strategic vision and semester programming for the chapter.
  • Coordinated and executed campus events including professional panels, workshops, and networking sessions connecting students with industry leaders.
  • Built and maintained corporate partnerships with firms like X-Square Capital, Sygnus Capital, Merrill Lynch, CAF Investment, and Parallel18, facilitating site visits, talks, and mentorship opportunities.
  • Represented FMA members in meetings with university administration and Business department faculty.
  • Oversaw chapter operations including officer coordination, budgeting, event logistics, and membership recruitment.
  • Created internship and networking pipelines for members by fostering relationships with alumni and corporate contacts in the finance sector.
FMA Board of Directors
